015 */
016package org.consensusj.bitcoin.jsonrpc;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JavaType;
019import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode;
020import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.type.TypeFactory;
021import org.bitcoinj.base.BitcoinNetwork;
022import org.bitcoinj.base.LegacyAddress;
023import org.bitcoinj.base.Network;
024import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.conversion.HexUtil;
025import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.AddressGroupingItem;
026import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.AddressInfo;
027import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.BitcoinTransactionInfo;
028import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.BlockChainInfo;
029import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.BlockInfo;
030import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.ChainTip;
031import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.LoadWalletResult;
032import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.MethodHelpEntry;
033import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.MinimalDescriptor;
034import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.NetworkInfo;
035import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.Outpoint;
036import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.RawTransactionInfo;
037import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.ReceivedByAddressInfo;
038import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.SignedRawTransaction;
039import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.TxOutInfo;
040import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.TxOutSetInfo;
041import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.UnloadWalletResult;
042import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.UnspentOutput;
043import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.conversion.RpcClientModule;
044import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.WalletTransactionInfo;
045import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.ZmqNotification;
046import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.bitcore.AddressBalanceInfo;
047import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.bitcore.AddressRequest;
048import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.bitcore.AddressUtxoInfo;
049import org.consensusj.bitcoin.jsonrpc.internal.BitcoinClientThreadFactory;
050import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.DefaultRpcClient;
051import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcError;
052import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcErrorException;
053import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcException;
054import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcMessage;
055import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcRequest;
056import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcResponse;
057import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcStatusException;
058import org.bitcoinj.base.Address;
059import org.bitcoinj.core.Block;
060import org.bitcoinj.base.Coin;
061import org.bitcoinj.core.Context;
062import org.bitcoinj.crypto.ECKey;
063import org.bitcoinj.base.Sha256Hash;
064import org.bitcoinj.core.Transaction;
065import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcTransport;
066import org.slf4j.Logger;
067import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
068
069import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
070import java.io.EOFException;
071import java.io.IOException;
072import java.net.ConnectException;
073import java.net.SocketException;
074import java.net.URI;
075import java.nio.ByteBuffer;
076import java.time.Duration;
077import java.time.Instant;
078import java.util.List;
079import java.util.Map;
080import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
081import java.util.concurrent.CompletionException;
082import java.util.concurrent.ExecutorService;
083import java.util.concurrent.Executors;
084import java.util.concurrent.ThreadFactory;
085import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
086import java.util.concurrent.TimeoutException;
087import java.util.function.Function;
088import java.util.function.Supplier;
089import java.util.stream.Collectors;
090import java.util.stream.Stream;
091
092/**
093 * JSON-RPC Client for <b>Bitcoin Core</b>.
094 * <p>
095 * A strongly-typed wrapper for a Bitcoin Core JSON-RPC client using the
096 * <a href="https://bitcoincore.org/en/doc/">Bitcoin Core JSON-RPC API</a>.
097 * <p>
098 * <a href="https://bitcoinj.org">bitcoinj</a> types are used where appropriate.
099 * For example, requesting a block hash will return a {@link org.bitcoinj.base.Sha256Hash}:
100 *
101 * <pre> {@code
102 * Sha256Hash hash = client.getBlockHash(342650);
103 * }</pre>
104 *
105 * Requesting a Bitcoin balance will return the amount as a {@link org.bitcoinj.base.Coin}:
106 *
107 * <pre> {@code
108 * Coin balance = client.getBalance();
109 * }</pre>
110 *
111 * This version is written to be compatible with Bitcoin Core v26.0 and later.
112 * <p>
113 * Note that according to <a href="https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues/2960">Issue #2960: Support JSON-RPC 2.0</a> Bitcoin Core
114 * does not correctly follow the JSON-RPC 2.0 specification.
115 * @see org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcClient
116 * @see <a href="https://bitcoincore.org/en/doc/">Bitcoin Core JSON-RPC API reference</a>
117 * @see <a href="https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues/2960">Bitcoin Core Issue #2960: Support JSON-RPC 2.0</a>
118 * <p>
119 * <b>This is still a work-in-progress and the API will change.</b>
120 *
121 */
122public class BitcoinClient extends DefaultRpcClient implements ChainTipClient {
123    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(BitcoinClient.class);
124
125    private static final int THREAD_POOL_SIZE = 5;
126
127    // Delay between retries in waitForServer and waitForBlock
128    private static final Duration RETRY_DELAY = Duration.ofSeconds(5);
129    // Delay between log messages in waitForBlock
130    private static final Duration MESSAGE_DELAY = Duration.ofSeconds(30);
131
132    public static final int BITCOIN_CORE_VERSION_MIN = 200000;              // Minimum Bitcoin Core supported (tested) version
133    public static final int BITCOIN_CORE_VERSION_DESC_DEFAULT = 230000;     // Bitcoin Core version that DEFAULTS to descriptor wallets
134
135    // CompletableFuture<Network> is used to handle optional lazy (from-remote) initialization 
136    private final CompletableFuture<Network> fNetwork = new CompletableFuture<>();
137    // This tells us that we have successfully connected to the server at least once via any of the methods
138    // that eventually call waitForBlockchainInfoAsync()
139    private final CompletableFuture<Void> fConnectedOnce = new CompletableFuture<>();
140    private final ExecutorService executorService;
141
142    private int serverVersion = 0;    // 0 means unknown serverVersion
143    private boolean isAddressIndexSuccessfullyTested = false;
144    private boolean isAddressIndexEnabled;
145
146    public BitcoinClient(SSLContext sslContext, Network network, URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword) {
147        super(sslContext, JsonRpcMessage.Version.V2, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword);
148        if (network != null) {
149            this.fNetwork.complete(network); // Non-lazy initialization case
150        }
151        ThreadFactory threadFactory = new BitcoinClientThreadFactory(new Context(), "Bitcoin RPC Client");
152        // TODO: Tune and/or make configurable the thread pool size.
153        // Current pool size of 5 is chosen to minimize simultaneous active RPC
154        // calls in `bitcoind` -- which is not designed for serving multiple clients.
155        executorService = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(THREAD_POOL_SIZE, threadFactory);
156        mapper.registerModule(new RpcClientModule());
157    }
158
159    // TODO: Reconcile this constructor mode with {@link #waitForServer(int)}
160    /**
161     * Incubating constructor that doesn't require a {@link Network}.
162     * <p>
163     * When using this constructor, it is recommended that {@link #getNetwork()} be called after construction
164     * and before any other methods are called, to allow the Bitcoin network type to be initialized.
165     * @param sslContext Custom socket factory
166     * @param server URI of the Bitcoin RPC server
167     * @param rpcuser Username (if required)
168     * @param rpcpassword Password (if required)
169     */
170    public BitcoinClient(SSLContext sslContext, URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword) {
171        this(sslContext, null, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword);
172        // Lazy initialization of network. Should we send a request from the constructor?
173    }
174
175    /**
176     * Incubating constructor that doesn't require a {@link Network}.
177     * <p>
178     * When using this constructor, it is recommended that {@link #getNetwork()} be called after construction
179     * and before any other methods are called, to allow the Bitcoin network type to be initialized.
180     * @param server URI of the Bitcoin RPC server
181     * @param rpcuser Username (if required)
182     * @param rpcpassword Password (if required)
183     */
184    public BitcoinClient(URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword) {
185        this(JsonRpcTransport.getDefaultSSLContext(), server, rpcuser, rpcpassword);
186    }
187
188    /**
189     * Construct a BitcoinClient from Network, URI, user name, and password.
190     * @param network Correct Network Parameters for destination server
191     * @param server URI of the Bitcoin RPC server
192     * @param rpcuser Username (if required)
193     * @param rpcpassword Password (if required)
194     */
195    public BitcoinClient(Network network, URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword) {
196        this(JsonRpcTransport.getDefaultSSLContext(), network, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword);
197    }
198
199    /**
200     * Construct a BitcoinClient from an RPCConfig data object.
201     * @param config Contains URI, user name, and password
202     */
203    public BitcoinClient(RpcConfig config) {
204        this(config.network(), config.getURI(), config.getUsername(), config.getPassword());
205    }
206
207    /**
208     * Get network.
209     * <p>
210     * Historically the Bitcoin network has been required as a constructor parameter and required to match
211     * the mode of the server. However, to simplify client configuration we have added a constructor
212     * that doesn't require specification of a network. This changes some assumptions about how {@code BitcoinClient} works.
213     * Previously, no JSON-RPC I/O calls would be performed unless something was explicitly requested -- which
214     * also gave users of {@code BitcoinClient} the ability to call {@link #waitForServer(Duration)}
215     * before calling any RPCs.
216     * <p>
217     * Until further improvements/changes are made, if you use one of the constructors that does not specify a
218     * {@code Network} you should call {@link #connectToServer(Duration)} as soon as possible after calling the constructor
219     * (especially before calling any JSON-RPC I/O methods.
220     * <p>
221     *  Well-behaved users of {@code BitcoinClient} should only call this method <i>after</i> either providing
222     *  a {@link Network} in the constructor or calling one of the methods that makes a connection. Because this
223     *  method is also used internally and indirectly, there is a short timeout specified for extra safety.
224     * @return network for the server
225     */
226    public synchronized Network getNetwork() {
227        // TODO: Should this auto-connect?
228        return fNetwork.orTimeout(1, TimeUnit.MINUTES).join();
229    }
230
231    @Override
232    public ExecutorService getDefaultAsyncExecutor() {
233        return executorService;
234    }
235    
236    /**
237     * Shutdown our thread pool, etc.
238     */
239    @Override
240    public void close() {
241        // TODO: See shutdownAndAwaitTermination method in the ExecutorService JavaDoc for how to correctly implement this.
242        executorService.shutdown();
243        boolean successfullyTerminated;
244        try {
245            successfullyTerminated = executorService.awaitTermination(10, TimeUnit.SECONDS);
246        } catch (InterruptedException e) {
247            throw new RuntimeException(e);
248        }
249        if (!successfullyTerminated) {
250            log.warn("timeout while closing");
251        }
252    }
253
254    /**
255     * Get a (cached after first call) serverVersion number
256     * @return serverVersion version of bitcoin server node
257     */
258    synchronized int getServerVersion() {
259        if (serverVersion == 0) {
260            try {
261                serverVersion = getNetworkInfo().getVersion();
262            } catch (IOException e) {
263                throw new RuntimeException(e);
264            }
265            if (serverVersion < BITCOIN_CORE_VERSION_MIN) {
266                throw new RuntimeException("Unsupported server version");
267            }
268        }
269        return serverVersion;
270    }
271
272    /**
273     * Get the {@link Network} for this client by asking the server.
274     * @return A future for the server-side {@code Network}
275     */
276    private CompletableFuture<Network> getNetworkFromServer() {
277        return getBlockChainInfoAsync().thenApply(BlockChainInfo::chainToNetwork);
278    }
279
280    /**
281     * Test if address index is enabled, caching the result of the first successful check
282     *
283     * @return true if enabled, false if not enabled
284     * @throws JsonRpcException an exception other than 1 of the two expected exceptions is thrown
285     * @throws IOException something else went wrong
286     */
287    public synchronized boolean isAddressIndexEnabled() throws JsonRpcException, IOException {
288        if (!isAddressIndexSuccessfullyTested) {
289            try {
290                AddressBalanceInfo info = getAddressBalance(getTestAddress());
291                isAddressIndexSuccessfullyTested = true;
292                isAddressIndexEnabled = true;
293            } catch (JsonRpcErrorException ee) {
294                // If method not found, the method we use for the test isn't even present, so definitely
295                // no address index support is available
296                if (ee.getError().getCode() == JsonRpcError.Error.METHOD_NOT_FOUND.getCode()) {
297                    isAddressIndexSuccessfullyTested = true;
298                    isAddressIndexEnabled = false;
299                } else {
300                    // Some other, unexpected exception, throw it
301                    throw ee;
302                }
303            } catch (JsonRpcStatusException se) {
304                // If the method is there and it returns an error of "Address index not enabled" then we also
305                // know that no address index support is available
306                if (se.getMessage().equals("Address index not enabled")) {
307                    isAddressIndexSuccessfullyTested = true;
308                    isAddressIndexEnabled = false;
309                } else if ( se.jsonRpcCode == JsonRpcError.Error.METHOD_NOT_FOUND.getCode()) {
310                    // It looks like the behavior changed in Bitcoin Core v23.0 and we end up here rather than in the  JsonRpcErrorException handler above
311                    isAddressIndexSuccessfullyTested = true;
312                    isAddressIndexEnabled = false;
313                } else {
314                    // Some other, unexpected exception, throw it
315                    throw se;
316                }
317            }
318        }
319        return isAddressIndexEnabled;
320    }
321
322    private Address getTestAddress() {
323        BitcoinNetwork network = (BitcoinNetwork) getNetwork();
324        switch (network) {
325            case MAINNET:
326                return LegacyAddress.fromBase58( "1A1zP1eP5QGefi2DMPTfTL5SLmv7DivfNa", network);
327            case TESTNET:
328            case REGTEST:
329            case SIGNET:
330            default:
331                return LegacyAddress.fromBase58( "moneyqMan7uh8FqdCA2BV5yZ8qVrc9ikLP", network);
332        }
333    }
334
335    /**
336     * Wait until the server is available.
337     * <p>
338     * Keep trying, ignoring (and logging) a known list of exception conditions that may occur while waiting for
339     * a {@code bitcoind} server to start up. This is similar to the behavior enabled by the {@code -rpcwait}
340     * option to the {@code bitcoin-cli} command-line tool.
341     *
342     * @param timeoutSeconds Timeout in seconds
343     * @return true if ready, false if timeout or interrupted
344     * @throws JsonRpcException if an "unexpected" exception happens (i.e. an error other than what happens during normal server startup)
345     * @deprecated Use {@link #waitForServer(Duration)}
346     */
347    @Deprecated
348    public boolean waitForServer(int timeoutSeconds) throws JsonRpcException {
349        return waitForServer(Duration.ofSeconds(timeoutSeconds));
350    }
351
352    /**
353     * Poll the server regularly until it responds.
354     * @param timeout how long to wait for server
355     * @return Completes with {@code true} if server responded, {@code false} for timeout
356     * @throws JsonRpcException if an unexpected JsonRpcException occurs
357     */
358    public boolean waitForServer(Duration timeout) throws JsonRpcException {
359        try {
360            return syncGet(waitForServerAsync(timeout, RETRY_DELAY));
361        } catch (JsonRpcException je) {
362            throw je;
363        } catch (IOException ioe) {
364            throw new RuntimeException(ioe);
365        }
366    }
367
368    /**
369     * Actively connect to server (one time) waiting for it to start if necessary
370     * @param timeout how long to wait
371     * @return completes successfully on connection or exceptionally on fatal error or timeout
372     */
373    public CompletableFuture<Void> connectToServer(Duration timeout) {
374        return waitForBlockchainInfoAsync(timeout, RETRY_DELAY)
375                .thenApply(info -> null);
376    }
377
378    /**
379     * This is like waitForServer but does not initiate a connection. It depends upon
380     * something else having done so.
381     * @return a future (possibly already completed) that indicates we have connected at least once to the server
382     */
383    public CompletableFuture<Void> waitForConnected() {
384        return fConnectedOnce.minimalCompletionStage().toCompletableFuture();
385    }
386
387    public CompletableFuture<Void> waitForConnected(Duration timeout) {
388        return waitForConnected().orTimeout(timeout.toSeconds(), TimeUnit.SECONDS);
389    }
390
391    // TODO: Consider renaming this method "connect"
392    // TODO: Consider having this method automatically get the Bitcoin Network, Server Version, RegTest mining address, etc once connection is made (the generic waitForServer() method is capable of doing that)
393    /**
394     * Wait for a connection to the server. Uses {@code getblockchaininfo} to poll server.
395     * @param timeout how long to wait for server
396     * @param retry time to wait between retries
397     * @return Completes with {@code true} if server responded, {@code false} for timeout, exceptionally for unexpected errors.
398     */
399    protected CompletableFuture<Boolean> waitForServerAsync(Duration timeout, Duration retry) {
400        return waitForBlockchainInfoAsync(timeout, retry)
401                .handle((i, t) -> {
402                    if (i != null) {
403                        return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(Boolean.TRUE);
404                    } else if (t instanceof TimeoutException) {
405                        return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(Boolean.FALSE);
406                    } else {
407                        return CompletableFuture.<Boolean>failedFuture(t);
408                    }
409                })
410                .thenCompose(Function.identity());
411    }
412
413    protected CompletableFuture<BlockChainInfo> waitForBlockchainInfoAsync(Duration timeout, Duration retry) {
414        Supplier<JsonRpcRequest> requestSupplier = () -> buildJsonRequest("getblockchaininfo");
415        CompletableFuture<BlockChainInfo> cf = waitForServer(timeout, retry, requestSupplier, typeForClass(BlockChainInfo.class), this::mapBitcoinTransientErrors);
416        return cf.whenComplete((info, t) -> {
417            if (info != null) {
418                fConnectedOnce.complete(null);
419                fNetwork.complete(BlockChainInfo.chainToNetwork(info));
420            }
421        });
422    }
423
424    /**
425     * Wait for RPC server to reach specified block height.
426     *
427     * @param blockHeight Block height to wait for
428     * @param timeout     Timeout in seconds
429     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
430     * @throws IOException network error
431     * @return True if blockHeight reached, false if timeout or interrupted
432     */
433    public Boolean waitForBlock(int blockHeight, int timeout) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
434
435        log.info("Waiting for server to reach block " + blockHeight);
436
437        long seconds = 0;
438        while (seconds < timeout) {
439            Integer block = this.getBlockCount();
440            if (block >= blockHeight) {
441                log.info("Server is at block " + block + " returning 'true'.");
442                return true;
443            } else {
444                try {
445                    if (seconds % MESSAGE_DELAY.toSeconds() == 0) {
446                        log.debug("Server at block " + block);
447                    }
448                    Thread.sleep(RETRY_DELAY.toMillis());
449                    seconds += RETRY_DELAY.toSeconds();
450                } catch (InterruptedException e) {
451                    log.error(e.toString());
452                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
453                    return false;
454                }
455            }
456        }
457
458        log.error("Timeout waiting for block " + blockHeight);
459        return false;
460    }
461
462    /**
463     * TransientErrorMapper suitable for waiting for a temporarily down or restarting Bitcoin JSON-RPC server. This
464     * can be used to implement the {@code -rpcwait} command-line option, for instance.
465     */
466    protected  <T> CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<T>> mapBitcoinTransientErrors(JsonRpcRequest request, JsonRpcResponse<T> response, Throwable t) {
467        if (response != null) {
468            return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(response);
469        } else {
470            if (t instanceof CompletionException) {
471                // Java.net.http driver
472                if (t.getCause() != null) {
473                    if (t.getCause() instanceof ConnectException) {
474                        return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(temporarilyUnavailableResponse(request, t));
475                    } else {
476                        return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(t);
477                    }
478                }
479            }
480            if (t instanceof SocketException) {
481                // Java HttpUrlConnection driver
482                SocketException se = (SocketException) t;
483                // These are expected exceptions while waiting for a server
484                if (se.getMessage().equals("Unexpected end of file from server") ||
485                        se.getMessage().equals("Connection reset") ||
486                        se.getMessage().contains("Connection refused") ||
487                        se.getMessage().equals("recvfrom failed: ECONNRESET (Connection reset by peer)")) {
488                    // Transient error, generate synthetic JSON-RPC error response with appropriate values
489                    return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(temporarilyUnavailableResponse(request, t));
490                } else {
491                    return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(se);
492                }
493            } else if (t instanceof EOFException) {
494                /* Android exception, ignore */
495                // Expected exceptions on Android, RoboVM
496                return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(temporarilyUnavailableResponse(request, t));
497            } else if (t instanceof JsonRpcStatusException) {
498                JsonRpcStatusException e = (JsonRpcStatusException) t;
499                // If server is in "warm-up" mode, e.g. validating/parsing the blockchain...
500                if (e.jsonRpcCode == -28) {
501                    // ...then grab text message for status logging
502                    return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(temporarilyUnavailableResponse(request, t));
503                } else {
504                    return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(e);
505                }
506            } else if (t instanceof IOException) {
507                // Ignore all IOExceptions
508                return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(temporarilyUnavailableResponse(request, t));
509            } else {
510                return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(t);
511            }
512        }
513    }
514
515    /**
516     * Tell the server to stop
517     * @return A status string
518     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
519     * @throws IOException network error
520     */
521    public String stop() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
522        return (String) send("stop");
523    }
524
525    /**
526     * Returns the number of blocks in the longest block chain.
527     *
528     * @return The current block count
529     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
530     * @throws IOException network error
531     */
532    public Integer getBlockCount() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
533        return (Integer) send("getblockcount");
534    }
535
536    /**
537     * Returns the hash of block in best-block-chain at index provided.
538     *
539     * @param index The block index
540     * @return The block hash
541     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
542     * @throws IOException network error
543     */
544    public Sha256Hash getBlockHash(Integer index) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
545        return send("getblockhash", Sha256Hash.class, index);
546    }
547
548    /**
549     * Returns information about a block with the given block hash.
550     *
551     * @param hash The block hash
552     * @return The information about the block (JSON/POJO object)
553     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
554     * @throws IOException network error
555     */
556    public BlockInfo getBlockInfo(Sha256Hash hash) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
557        // Use "verbose = true"
558        return send("getblock", BlockInfo.class, hash, true);
559    }
560
561    /**
562     * Returns information about a block with the given block hash.
563     *
564     * @param hash The block hash
565     * @return The information about the block (bitcoinj {@link Block} object)
566     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
567     * @throws IOException network error
568     */
569    public Block getBlock(Sha256Hash hash) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
570        return syncGet(getBlockAsync(hash));
571    }
572
573    public CompletableFuture<Block> getBlockAsync(Sha256Hash hash) {
574        // Use "verbosity = 0" for raw block data
575        return sendAsync("getblock", Block.class, hash, 0);
576    }
577
578    /**
579     * Composite query that first calls ChainTips, then gets best block for the active tip
580     * @return The current best block
581     */
582    public CompletableFuture<Block> getBestBlock() {
583        return getChainTipsAsync()
584                .thenCompose(tips -> ChainTip.findActiveChainTip(tips)
585                        .map(tip -> getBlockAsync(tip.getHash()))
586                        .orElseGet(() -> CompletableFuture.failedFuture(new RuntimeException("No active ChainTip")))
587                );
588    }
589
590    /**
591     * Mine blocks immediately (RegTest mode)
592     * @since Bitcoin Core 0.13.0
593     *
594     * @param numBlocks Number of blocks to mine
595     * @param address Address to send mined coins to
596     * @param maxtries How many iterations to try (or null to use server default -- currently 1,000,000)
597     * @return list containing block header hashes of the generated blocks
598     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
599     * @throws IOException network error
600     */
601    public List<Sha256Hash> generateToAddress(int numBlocks, Address address, Integer maxtries) throws IOException {
602        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, Sha256Hash.class);
603        return (List<Sha256Hash>) send("generatetoaddress", resultType, (long) numBlocks, address, maxtries);
604    }
605
606    /**
607     * Mine blocks immediately (RegTest mode)
608     * @since Bitcoin Core 0.13.0
609     *
610     * @param numBlocks Number of blocks to mine
611     * @param address Address to send mined coins to
612     * @return list containing block header hashes of the generated blocks
613     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
614     * @throws IOException network error
615     */
616    public List<Sha256Hash> generateToAddress(int numBlocks, Address address) throws IOException {
617        return generateToAddress(numBlocks,address, null);
618    }
619
620    public List<String> listWallets() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
621        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, String.class);
622        return (List<String>) send("listwallets", resultType);
623    }
624
625    public LoadWalletResult createWallet(String name, Boolean disablePrivateKeys, Boolean blank) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
626        return createWallet(name, disablePrivateKeys, blank, null, null, null, null, null);
627    }
628
629    public LoadWalletResult createWallet(String name, Boolean disablePrivateKeys, Boolean blank, String passPhrase,
630                                         Boolean avoidReuse) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
631        return send("createwallet", LoadWalletResult.class, name, disablePrivateKeys, blank, passPhrase, avoidReuse);
632    }
633
634    // To use all these params server must be "recent"
635    public LoadWalletResult createWallet(String name, Boolean disablePrivateKeys, Boolean blank, String passPhrase,
636                                         Boolean avoidReuse, Boolean descriptors, Boolean loadOnStartup,
637                                         Boolean externalSigner) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
638        return send("createwallet", LoadWalletResult.class, name, disablePrivateKeys, blank, passPhrase, avoidReuse, descriptors, loadOnStartup, externalSigner);
639    }
640
641    public UnloadWalletResult unloadWallet() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
642        return unloadWallet(null, null);
643    }
644
645    public UnloadWalletResult unloadWallet(String name, Boolean loadOnStartup) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
646        return send("unloadwallet", UnloadWalletResult.class, name, loadOnStartup);
647    }
648
649    /**
650     * Creates a new Bitcoin address for receiving payments, linked to the default account "".
651     *
652     * @return A new Bitcoin address
653     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
654     * @throws IOException network error
655     */
656    public Address getNewAddress() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
657        return getNewAddress(null);
658    }
659
660    /**
661     * Creates a new Bitcoin address for receiving payments.
662     *
663     * @param label The label name for the address to be linked to.
664     * @return A new Bitcoin address
665     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
666     * @throws IOException network error
667     */
668    public Address getNewAddress(String label) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
669        return send("getnewaddress", Address.class, label);
670    }
671    
672    /**
673     * Return the private key from the server.
674     *
675     * Note: must be in wallet mode with unlocked or unencrypted wallet.
676     *
677     * @param address Address corresponding to the private key to return
678     * @return The private key
679     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
680     * @throws IOException network error
681     */
682    public ECKey dumpPrivKey(Address address) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
683        return send("dumpprivkey", ECKey.class, address);
684    }
685
686    /**
687     * Import a private key into the server's wallet
688     *
689     * @param privateKey An ECKey (containing a private key)
690     * @param label The server-side label for the key
691     * @param rescan Rescan the blockchain to find transactions for this key
692     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
693     * @throws IOException network error
694     */
695    public void importPrivKey(ECKey privateKey, String label, boolean rescan) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
696        send("importprivkey", Void.class, privateKey.getPrivateKeyEncoded(this.getNetwork()).toBase58(), label, rescan);
697    }
698
699    // Minimal descriptor support for RegTest mining (e.g. a single address, external)
700    public JsonNode importDescriptor(String descriptor, boolean active, Instant timeStamp, String label) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
701        MinimalDescriptor minimalDescriptor = new MinimalDescriptor(descriptor, false, timeStamp.getEpochSecond(), false);
702        List<MinimalDescriptor> descriptorList = List.of(minimalDescriptor);
703        log.warn("Descriptor list: {}", this.mapper.writeValueAsString(descriptorList));
704        return send("importdescriptors", JsonNode.class, descriptorList);
705    }
706
707    /**
708     * Creates a raw transaction spending the given inputs to the given destinations.
709     *
710     * Note: the transaction inputs are not signed, and the transaction is not stored in the wallet or transmitted to
711     * the network.
712     *
713     * @param inputs  The outpoints to spent
714     * @param outputs The destinations and amounts to transfer
715     * @return The hex-encoded raw transaction
716     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
717     * @throws IOException network error
718     */
719    public String createRawTransaction(List<Outpoint> inputs, Map<Address, Coin> outputs)
720            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
721        return (String) send("createrawtransaction", inputs, outputs);
722    }
723
724    /**
725     * Signs inputs of a raw transaction using the wallet. Arguments 2 and 3 of the RPC are currently
726     * not supported, which means UTXOs not currently in the blockchain can't be used and `sighashtype`
727     * defaults to `ALL`.
728     *
729     * @param unsignedTransaction The hex-encoded raw transaction
730     * @return The signed transaction and information whether it has a complete set of signature
731     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
732     * @throws IOException network error
733     * @since Bitcoin Core v0.17
734     */
735    public SignedRawTransaction signRawTransactionWithWallet(String unsignedTransaction) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
736        return send("signrawtransactionwithwallet", SignedRawTransaction.class, unsignedTransaction);
737    }
738
739    /**
740     * Get a "raw" transaction (which we use to construct a bitcoinj {@code Transaction})
741     * @param txid Transaction ID/hash
742     * @return bitcoinj Transaction
743     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
744     * @throws IOException network error
745     */
746    public Transaction getRawTransaction(Sha256Hash txid) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
747        String hexEncoded = (String) send("getrawtransaction", txid);
748        byte[] raw = HexUtil.hexStringToByteArray(hexEncoded);
749        return Transaction.read(ByteBuffer.wrap(raw));
750    }
751
752    /**
753     * Get a "raw" transaction as JSON (which we map to a RawTransactionInfo POJO)
754     * @param txid Transaction ID/hash
755     * @return RawTransactionInfo POJO
756     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
757     * @throws IOException network error
758     */
759    public RawTransactionInfo getRawTransactionInfo(Sha256Hash txid) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
760        return send("getrawtransaction", RawTransactionInfo.class, txid, 1);
761    }
762
763    /**
764     * Submit a raw transaction to local node and network
765     *
766     * @since Bitcoin Core 0.19
767     * @param tx The raw transaction
768     * @param maxFeeRate  Reject transactions whose fee rate is higher than this value, expressed in BTC/kB.
769     *                    Set to 0 to accept any fee rate.
770     * @param maxBurnAmount Any transaction containing an unspendable output with a value greater than maxburnamount will not be submitted.
771     * @return SHA256 Transaction ID
772     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
773     * @throws IOException network error
774     */
775    public Sha256Hash sendRawTransaction(Transaction tx, Coin maxFeeRate, Coin maxBurnAmount) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
776        return send("sendrawtransaction", Sha256Hash.class, tx, maxFeeRate, maxBurnAmount);
777    }
778
779    /**
780     * Submit a raw transaction to local node and network
781     *
782     * @since Bitcoin Core 0.19
783     * @param hexTx The raw transaction as a hex-encoded string
784     * @param maxFeeRate  Reject transactions whose fee rate is higher than this value, expressed in BTC/kB.
785     *                    Set to 0 to accept any fee rate.
786     * @param maxBurnAmount Any transaction containing an unspendable output with a value greater than maxburnamount will not be submitted.
787     * @return SHA256 Transaction ID
788     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
789     * @throws IOException network error
790     */
791    public Sha256Hash sendRawTransaction(String hexTx, Coin maxFeeRate, Coin maxBurnAmount) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
792        return send("sendrawtransaction", Sha256Hash.class, hexTx, maxFeeRate, maxBurnAmount);
793    }
794
795    /**
796     * Submit a raw transaction to local node and network using server's default `maxFeeRate`
797     *
798     * @param tx The raw transaction
799     * @return SHA256 Transaction ID
800     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
801     * @throws IOException network error
802     */
803    public Sha256Hash sendRawTransaction(Transaction tx) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
804        return sendRawTransaction(tx, null, null);
805    }
806
807    /**
808     * Submit a raw transaction to local node and network using server's default `maxFeeRate`
809     *
810     * @since Bitcoin Core 0.19
811     * @param hexTx The raw transaction as a hex-encoded string
812     * @return SHA256 Transaction ID
813     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
814     * @throws IOException network error
815     */
816    public Sha256Hash sendRawTransaction(String hexTx) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
817        return sendRawTransaction(hexTx, null, null);
818    }
819
820    public AddressInfo getAddressInfo(Address address) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
821        return send("getaddressinfo", AddressInfo.class, address);
822    }
823
824    public Coin getReceivedByAddress(Address address) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
825        return getReceivedByAddress(address, 1);   // Default to 1 or more confirmations
826    }
827
828    /**
829     * get total amount received by an address.
830     *
831     * @param address Address to query
832     * @param minConf minimum number of confirmations
833     * @return Is now returning `Coin`, if you need to convert use `BitcoinMath.btcToCoin(result)`
834     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
835     * @throws IOException network error
836     */
837    public Coin getReceivedByAddress(Address address, Integer minConf) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
838        return send("getreceivedbyaddress", Coin.class, address, minConf);
839    }
840
841    public List<ReceivedByAddressInfo> listReceivedByAddress(Integer minConf, Boolean includeEmpty)
842            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
843        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, ReceivedByAddressInfo.class);
844        return (List<ReceivedByAddressInfo>) send("listreceivedbyaddress", resultType, minConf, includeEmpty);
845    }
846
847    /**
848     * Returns a list of unspent transaction outputs with at least one confirmation.
849     *
850     * @return The unspent transaction outputs
851     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
852     * @throws IOException network error
853     */
854    public List<UnspentOutput> listUnspent() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
855        return listUnspent(null, null, null, null);
856    }
857
858    /**
859     * Returns a list of unspent transaction outputs with at least {@code minConf} and not more than {@code maxConf}
860     * confirmations.
861     *
862     * @param minConf The minimum confirmations to filter
863     * @param maxConf The maximum confirmations to filter
864     * @return The unspent transaction outputs
865     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
866     * @throws IOException network error
867     */
868    public List<UnspentOutput> listUnspent(Integer minConf, Integer maxConf)
869            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
870        return listUnspent(minConf, maxConf, null, null);
871    }
872
873    public List<UnspentOutput> listUnspent(Integer minConf, Integer maxConf, Address address)
874            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
875        return listUnspent(minConf, maxConf, List.of(address), true);
876    }
877
878    public List<UnspentOutput> listUnspent(Integer minConf, Integer maxConf, List<Address> filter)
879            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
880        return listUnspent(minConf, maxConf, filter, true);
881    }
882
883    /**
884     * Returns a list of unspent transaction outputs with at least {@code minConf} and not more than {@code maxConf}
885     * confirmations, filtered by a list of addresses.
886     *
887     * @param minConf The minimum confirmations to filter
888     * @param maxConf The maximum confirmations to filter
889     * @param filter  Include only transaction outputs to the specified addresses
890     * @param includeUnsafe optional
891     * @return The unspent transaction outputs
892     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
893     * @throws IOException network error
894     */
895    public List<UnspentOutput> listUnspent(Integer minConf, Integer maxConf, List<Address> filter, Boolean includeUnsafe)
896            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
897        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, UnspentOutput.class);
898        return (List<UnspentOutput>) send("listunspent", resultType, minConf, maxConf, filter, includeUnsafe);
899    }
900
901    /**
902     * Returns details about an unspent transaction output.
903     *
904     * @param txid The transaction hash
905     * @param vout The transaction output index
906     * @return Details about an unspent output or nothing, if the output was already spent
907     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
908     * @throws IOException network error
909     */
910    public TxOutInfo getTxOut(Sha256Hash txid, Integer vout) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
911        return getTxOut(txid, vout, null);
912    }
913
914
915    /**
916     * Returns details about an unspent transaction output.
917     *
918     * @param txid              The transaction hash
919     * @param vout              The transaction output index
920     * @param includeMemoryPool Whether to included the memory pool
921     * @return Details about an unspent output or nothing, if the output was already spent
922     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
923     * @throws IOException network error
924     */
925    public TxOutInfo getTxOut(Sha256Hash txid, Integer vout, Boolean includeMemoryPool)
926            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
927        return send("gettxout", TxOutInfo.class, txid, vout, includeMemoryPool);
928    }
929
930    /**
931     * Returns statistics about the unspent transaction output set.
932     * Note this call may take some time.
933     *
934     * @return statistics about the unspent transaction output set
935     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
936     * @throws IOException network error
937     */
938    public TxOutSetInfo getTxOutSetInfo() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
939        return send("gettxoutsetinfo", TxOutSetInfo.class);
940    }
941
942    /**
943     * Get the balance for a the default Bitcoin "account"
944     *
945     * @return Is now returning `Coin`, if you need to convert use `BitcoinMath.btcToCoin(result)`
946     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
947     * @throws IOException network error
948     */
949    public Coin getBalance() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
950        return getBalance(null, null);
951    }
952
953    /**
954     * Get the balance for a Bitcoin "account"
955     *
956     * @param account A Bitcoin "account". (Be wary of using this feature.)
957     * @return Is now returning `Coin`, if you need to convert use `BitcoinMath.btcToCoin(result)`
958     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
959     * @throws IOException network error
960     */
961    public Coin getBalance(String account) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
962        return getBalance(account, null);
963    }
964
965    /**
966     * Get the balance for a Bitcoin "account"
967     *
968     * @param account A Bitcoin "account". (Be wary of using this feature.)
969     * @param minConf minimum number of confirmations
970     * @return Is now returning `Coin`, if you need to convert use `BitcoinMath.btcToCoin(result)`
971     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
972     * @throws IOException network error
973     */
974    public Coin getBalance(String account, Integer minConf) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
975        return send("getbalance", Coin.class, account, minConf);
976    }
977
978    public Sha256Hash sendToAddress(Address address, Coin amount) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
979        return sendToAddress(address, amount, null, null);
980    }
981
982    public Sha256Hash sendToAddress(Address address, Coin amount, String comment, String commentTo)
983            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
984        return send("sendtoaddress", Sha256Hash.class, address, amount, comment, commentTo);
985    }
986
987    public Sha256Hash sendFrom(String account, Address address, Coin amount)
988            throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
989        return send("sendfrom", Sha256Hash.class, account, address, amount);
990    }
991
992    public Sha256Hash sendMany(String account, Map<Address, Coin> amounts) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
993        return send("sendmany", Sha256Hash.class, account, amounts);
994    }
995
996    /**
997     * Set the transaction fee per kB.
998     *
999     * @param amount The transaction fee in BTC/kB rounded to the nearest 0.00000001.
1000     * @return True if successful
1001     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1002     * @throws IOException network error
1003     */
1004    public Boolean setTxFee(Coin amount) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1005        return (Boolean) send("settxfee", amount);
1006    }
1007
1008    public WalletTransactionInfo getTransaction(Sha256Hash txid) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1009        return getTransaction(txid, null, null);
1010    }
1011
1012    public WalletTransactionInfo getTransaction(Sha256Hash txid, Boolean includeWatchOnly, Boolean verbose) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1013        return send("gettransaction", WalletTransactionInfo.class, txid, includeWatchOnly, verbose);
1014    }
1015
1016    /**
1017     * List the default number (10) of wallet transactions.
1018     * @return A list of wallet transactions
1019     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1020     * @throws IOException network error
1021     */
1022    public List<BitcoinTransactionInfo> listTransactions() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1023        return listTransactions(null, null, null, null);
1024    }
1025
1026    /**
1027     * List wallet transactions.
1028     * @param label Return transactions matching this address label, use "*" to return all transactions. {@code null} will use the server default ({@code "*"})
1029     * @param count Maximum number of transactions to return. {@code null} will use the sever default number (10)
1030     * @return A list of wallet transactions
1031     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1032     * @throws IOException network error
1033     */
1034    public List<BitcoinTransactionInfo> listTransactions(String label, Integer count) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1035        return listTransactions(label, count, null, null);
1036    }
1037    /**
1038     * List wallet transactions
1039     * @param label Return transactions matching this address label, use "*" to return all transactions. {@code null} will use the server default ({@code "*"})
1040     * @param count Maximum number of transactions to return. {@code null} will use the sever default number (10)
1041     * @param skip Number of transactions to skip. {@code null} will use the server default (0)
1042     * @param includeWatchOnly Include transactions to/from watch-only addresses. {@code null} will use the server default, which is  ({@code true}) for watch-only wallets, {@code false} otherwise.
1043     * @return A list of wallet transactions
1044     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1045     * @throws IOException network error
1046     */
1047    public List<BitcoinTransactionInfo> listTransactions(String label, Integer count, Integer skip, Boolean includeWatchOnly) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1048        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, BitcoinTransactionInfo.class);
1049        return (List<BitcoinTransactionInfo>) send("listtransactions", resultType, label, count, skip, includeWatchOnly);
1050    }
1051
1052    /**
1053     * The getblockchaininfo RPC provides information about the current state of the block chain.
1054     *
1055     * @return An object containing information about the current state of the block chain.
1056     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1057     * @throws IOException network error
1058     */
1059    public BlockChainInfo getBlockChainInfo() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1060        return syncGet(getBlockChainInfoAsync());
1061    }
1062
1063    public CompletableFuture<BlockChainInfo> getBlockChainInfoAsync() {
1064        return sendAsync("getblockchaininfo", BlockChainInfo.class);
1065    }
1066
1067    /**
1068     * The getnetworkinfo RPC returns information about the node's connection to the network.
1069     *
1070     * @return information about the node's connection to the network
1071     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1072     * @throws IOException network error
1073     */
1074    public NetworkInfo getNetworkInfo() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException  {
1075        return send("getnetworkinfo", NetworkInfo.class);
1076    }
1077
1078    /**
1079     * The {@code getzmqnotifications} RPC returns information about which configured ZMQ notifications are enabled
1080     * and on which ports.
1081     * 
1082     * @return A List of ZMQ Notification info records
1083     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1084     * @throws IOException network error
1085     */
1086    public List<ZmqNotification> getZmqNotifications() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException  {
1087        return syncGet(getZmqNotificationsAsync());
1088    }
1089
1090    public CompletableFuture<List<ZmqNotification>> getZmqNotificationsAsync() {
1091        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, ZmqNotification.class);
1092        return sendAsync("getzmqnotifications", resultType);
1093    }
1094
1095    /**
1096     * Returns list of related addresses.
1097     * Also useful for finding all change addresses in the wallet
1098     * @return a list of address groupings
1099     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1100     * @throws IOException network error
1101     */
1102    public List<List<AddressGroupingItem>>  listAddressGroupings() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1103        JavaType agiListType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, AddressGroupingItem.class);
1104        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, agiListType);
1105        return (List<List<AddressGroupingItem>>) send("listaddressgroupings", resultType);
1106    }
1107
1108    /**
1109     * Returns a human-readable list of available commands.
1110     * <p>
1111     * Bitcoin Core 0.10+ returns a categorized list of commands including blank lines
1112     * and header lines.
1113     *
1114     * @return The list of commands as string
1115     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1116     * @throws IOException network error
1117     */
1118    public String help() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1119        return help(null);
1120    }
1121
1122    /**
1123     * Returns a human-readable list of available commands.
1124     *
1125     * @return The response as a stream of one {@link String} for each line
1126     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1127     * @throws IOException network error
1128     */
1129    public Stream<String> helpAsStream() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1130        return lines(help());
1131    }
1132
1133    /**
1134     * Returns a human-readable list of available commands.
1135     *
1136     * @return The response as a list one {@link String} for each line
1137     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1138     * @throws IOException network error
1139     */
1140    public List<String> helpAsLines() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1141        return helpAsStream().collect(Collectors.toList());
1142    }
1143
1144    /**
1145     * Returns a human-readable list of available commands.
1146     *
1147     * @return The response as a list one {@link MethodHelpEntry} for each method
1148     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1149     * @throws IOException network error
1150     */
1151    public List<MethodHelpEntry> helpAsMethodEntries() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1152        return helpAsStream()
1153                .filter(this::isMethodEntry)
1154                .map(MethodHelpEntry::new)
1155                .collect(Collectors.toList());
1156    }
1157
1158    /**
1159     * Returns helpful information for a specific command.
1160     *
1161     * @param command The name of the command to get help for
1162     * @return The help text
1163     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1164     * @throws IOException network error
1165     */
1166    public String help(String command) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1167        return (String) send("help", command);
1168    }
1169
1170    /**
1171     * Returns a list of available commands.
1172     * <p>
1173     * Commands which are unavailable will not be listed, such as wallet RPCs, if wallet support is disabled.
1174     *
1175     * @return The list of commands
1176     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1177     * @throws IOException network error
1178     */
1179    public List<String> getCommands() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1180        return helpAsMethodEntries().stream()
1181                .map(MethodHelpEntry::getMethodName)
1182                .collect(Collectors.toList());
1183    }
1184
1185    private Stream<String> lines(String string) {
1186        // In JDK 11 this can be replaced by String::lines
1187        return Stream.of(string.split("\n"));
1188    }
1189
1190    private boolean isMethodEntry(String line) {
1191        // Filter out blank and header lines
1192        return !line.isEmpty() && !line.matches("== (.+) ==");
1193    }
1194
1195    /**
1196     * Checks whether a command exists.
1197     * <p>
1198     * This is done indirectly, by using {help(String) help} to get information about the command, and if information
1199     * about the command is available, then the command exists. The absence of information does not necessarily imply
1200     * the non-existence of a command.
1201     *
1202     * @param command The name of the command to check
1203     * @return True if the command exists
1204     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1205     * @throws IOException network error
1206     */
1207    public Boolean commandExists(String command) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1208        return !help(command).contains("help: unknown command");
1209    }
1210
1211    /**
1212     * Permanently marks a block as invalid, as if it violated a consensus rule.
1213     *
1214     * @param hash The block hash
1215     * @since Bitcoin Core 0.10
1216     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1217     * @throws IOException network error
1218     */
1219    public void invalidateBlock(Sha256Hash hash) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1220        send("invalidateblock", hash);
1221    }
1222
1223    /**
1224     * Removes invalidity status of a block and its descendants, reconsider them for activation.
1225     * <p>
1226     * This can be used to undo the effects of {link invalidateBlock(Sha256Hash) invalidateBlock}.
1227     *
1228     * @param hash The hash of the block to reconsider
1229     * @since Bitcoin Core 0.10
1230     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1231     * @throws IOException network error
1232     */
1233    public void reconsiderBlock(Sha256Hash hash) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1234        send("reconsiderblock", hash);
1235    }
1236
1237    /**
1238     * Return information about all known tips in the block tree, including the main chain as well as orphaned branches.
1239     *
1240     * @return A list of chain tip information
1241     * @since Bitcoin Core 0.10
1242     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1243     * @throws IOException network error
1244     * @deprecated Use {@link #getChainTipsAsync()}
1245     */
1246    @Override
1247    public List<ChainTip> getChainTips() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1248        return syncGet(getChainTipsAsync());
1249    }
1250
1251    @Override
1252    public CompletableFuture<List<ChainTip>> getChainTipsAsync() {
1253        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, ChainTip.class);
1254        return sendAsync("getchaintips", resultType);
1255    }
1256
1257    /**
1258     * Attempt to add or remove a node from the addnode list, or to try a connection to a node once.
1259     *
1260     * @param node node to add as a string in the form of {@code IP_address:port}
1261     * @param command `add`, `remove`, or `onetry`
1262     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1263     * @throws IOException network error
1264     */
1265    public void addNode(String node, String command) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
1266        send("addnode", node, command);
1267    }
1268
1269    /**
1270     * Return information about the given added node
1271     *
1272     * @param details `true` to return detailed information
1273     * @param node the node to provide information about
1274     * @return A Jackson JsonNode object (until we define a POJO)
1275     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1276     * @throws IOException network error
1277     */
1278    public JsonNode getAddedNodeInfo(boolean details, String node) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException  {
1279        return send("getaddednodeinfo", JsonNode.class, details, node);
1280    }
1281
1282    /**
1283     * Return information about all added nodes
1284     *
1285     * @param details `true` to return detailed information
1286     * @return A Jackson JsonNode object (until we define a POJO)
1287     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
1288     * @throws IOException network error
1289     */
1290    public JsonNode getAddedNodeInfo(boolean details) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException  {
1291        return getAddedNodeInfo(details, null);
1292    }
1293
1294    // Bitcore/Omni transaction for getting non-wallet address balances
1295    public AddressBalanceInfo getAddressBalance(Address address) throws JsonRpcException, IOException {
1296        return send("getaddressbalance", AddressBalanceInfo.class, address);
1297    }
1298
1299    // Bitcore/Omni transaction for getting non-wallet address balances
1300    public AddressBalanceInfo getAddressBalance(List<Address> addressList) throws JsonRpcException, IOException {
1301        return send("getaddressbalance", AddressBalanceInfo.class, new AddressRequest(addressList));
1302    }
1303
1304    // Bitcore/Omni transaction for getting non-wallet address UTXO Info
1305    public List<AddressUtxoInfo>  getAddressUtxos(Address address) throws JsonRpcException, IOException {
1306        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, AddressUtxoInfo.class);
1307        return (List<AddressUtxoInfo>) send("getaddressutxos", resultType, address);
1308    }
1309
1310    // Bitcore/Omni transaction for getting non-wallet address UTXO Info
1311    public List<AddressUtxoInfo> getAddressUtxos(List<Address> addressList) throws JsonRpcException, IOException {
1312        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, AddressUtxoInfo.class);
1313        return (List<AddressUtxoInfo>) send("getaddressutxos", resultType, new AddressRequest(addressList));
1314    }
1315}
